title: ASX Limited — Government accountability
investigation_objective: The Federal Court ordered ASX Limited, operator of Australia's national securities exchange, to pay a $20.5 million penalty after admitting a 10 February 2022 market announcement stating its CHESS replacement project was 'progressing well' was misleading, in contravention of the ASIC Act. Six weeks after the announcement ASX acknowledged a strong likelihood of delays, and by November 2022 the project (intended to replace critical clearing/settlement infrastructure using distributed ledger technology) was paused entirely, with ASX writing off approximately $245-255 million in project costs. ASX was also ordered to pay $3 million toward ASIC's costs.
scope: Federal Court civil penalty of $20.5 million, admitted, for misleading conduct concerning critical market infrastructure; Misleading statement concerned a project underpinning Australia's securities clearing and settlement system; Project was subsequently paused with approximately $245-255 million in costs written off
